욥기 4:2.content: “If someone ventures to talk with you, will you be grieved? But who can withhold himself from speaking?

욥기 4:3.content: Behold, you have instructed many, you have strengthened the weak hands.

욥기 4:4.content: Your words have supported him who was falling, You have made firm the feeble knees.

욥기 4:5.content: But now it has come to you, and you faint. It touches you, and you are troubled.

욥기 4:6.content: Isn’t your piety your confidence? Isn’t the integrity of your ways your hope?

욥기 4:7.content: “Remember, now, whoever perished, being innocent? Or where were the upright cut off?

욥기 4:8.content: According to what I have seen, those who plow iniquity, and sow trouble, reap the same.

욥기 4:9.content: By the breath of God they perish. By the blast of his anger are they consumed.

욥기 4:10.content: The roaring of the lion, and the voice of the fierce lion, the teeth of the young lions, are broken.

욥기 4:11.content: The old lion perishes for lack of prey. The cubs of the lioness are scattered abroad.

욥기 4:12.content: “Now a thing was secretly brought to me. My ear received a whisper of it.

욥기 4:13.content: In thoughts from the visions of the night, when deep sleep falls on men,

욥기 4:14.content: fear came on me, and trembling, which made all my bones shake.

욥기 4:15.content: Then a spirit passed before my face. The hair of my flesh stood up.

욥기 4:16.content: It stood still, but I couldn’t discern its appearance. A form was before my eyes. Silence, then I heard a voice, saying,

욥기 4:17.content: ‘Shall mortal man be more just than God? Shall a man be more pure than his Maker?

욥기 4:18.content: Behold, he puts no trust in his servants. He charges his angels with error.

욥기 4:19.content: How much more, those who dwell in houses of clay, whose foundation is in the dust, who are crushed before the moth!

욥기 4:20.content: Between morning and evening they are destroyed. They perish forever without any regarding it.

욥기 4:21.content: Isn’t their tent cord plucked up within them? They die, and that without wisdom.’
